channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Touchdown Tommy! Montana State's Mellott wins Walter Payton Award as top FCS offensive player
item.description: FRISCO, Texas (AP) — Montana State quarterback Tommy Mellott won the Walter Payton Award as the Football Championship Subdivision offensive player of the year Saturday night, two days before the top-ranked Bobcats face North Dakota State in the title game.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Chicago Bulls plan to retire Derrick Rose's jersey next season
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Derrick Rose wasn't sure how he will feel when sees his No. 1 hanging from the rafters. He was still trying to process the news. The Chicago Bulls announced Saturday they plan to retire Rose's jersey sometime next season.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ravens wrap up the AFC North with a 35-10 rout of Cleveland; Jackson surpasses 4,000 yards passing
item.description: Lamar Jackson threw two touchdown passes, and the Baltimore Ravens won the AFC North for the second straight season, clinching the division with a 35-10 victory over the Cleveland Browns.
item.dc:creator: Noah Trister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: BYU gets trounced by #14 Houston 86-55
item.description: HOUSTON (ABC4 Sports) - The BYU basketball team came into the Big 12 season thinking it could compete with the top teams in one of the toughest leagues in the nation. But if Saturday was any indication, the Cougars have a long way to go.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Livvy Dunne impresses in LSU's season opener with boyfriend Paul Skenes in attendance
item.description: Livvy Dunne began her quest to become a back-to-back national champion Friday night, and her LSU alum boyfriend Paul Skenes was in attendance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chiefs finally depart KC for Denver amid ice storm after 4 hours spent waiting on the tarmac
item.description: The Chiefs finally departed from Kansas City International Airport for their game in Denver on Saturday after spending about four hours stranded on the tarmac amid an ice storm that blanketed the region ahead of an impending blizzard.
item.dc:creator: Dave Skretta, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Aaron Rodgers fined $11,255 ahead of finale of Jets' abysmal season
item.description: The NFL fined quarterback Aaron Rodgers over $11,000 for a late hit against the Bills ahead of the New York Jets' final game of a tumultuous season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Orleans Saints donate $1 million to relief following terror attack near stadium
item.description: The New Orleans Saints announced on Saturday they will donate $1 million to relief funds for the victims of the terror attack that occurred near their stadium.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Father of Georgia football coach Kirby Smart dies after fall before Sugar Bowl
item.description: Kirby Smart's father, Sonny Smart, succumbed to complications related to surgery after he fell in New Orleans on New Year's Day before the Sugar Bowl, the university confirmed Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jeremiyah Love is expected to be ready for 7th-seeded Notre Dame in the Orange Bowl
item.description: Notre Dame coach Marcus Freeman expects running back Jeremiyah Love to play in Thursday's College Football Playoff semifinal game and the Fighting Irish ground game to be at full strength against sixth-seeded Penn State.
item.dc:creator: Michael Marot, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bulls to retire Derrick Rose's No. 1 jersey
item.description: CHICAGO — The Chicago Bulls announced Saturday that the team would be retiring Derrick Rose’s No. 1 jersey next season. Bulls President & CEO Michael Reinsdorf made the announcement on Saturday morning.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, WGN 9

item.title: LeBron James breaks Michael Jordan's record for 30-point games with his 563rd
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — LeBron James broke Michael Jordan's NBA record for 30-point games during the Los Angeles Lakers' victory over Atlanta on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bill keeping trans athletes out of women's sports will be heard by Congress after new rules package passes
item.description: The new Republican-controlled Congress will hear arguments on a bill that would prohibit transgender athletes from competing in women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lions' Amon-Ra St. Brown wants changes to NFL playoff format ahead of game for top seed
item.description: With the Detroit Lions at risk of being the first 14-win team to be a wild-card seed, Amon-Ra St. Brown says the NFL should change its playoff format.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Olympic sprinter faces domestic violence, robbery charges from 2024 incident after getting tased during arrest
item.description: U.S. Olympic sprinter and 2024 bronze medalist Fred Kerley was arrested and tased by police. Then, he was issued charges from a 2024 incident.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Charlie Morton agrees to a 1-year, $15 million deal with the Orioles
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Veteran right-hander Charlie Morton and the Baltimore Orioles agreed to a one-year, $15 million contract on Friday. The 41-year-old Morton, the hero of Game 7 of the 2017 World Series with Houston, will try to fill a hole in Baltimore's rotation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dodgers sign star out of Korean baseball league
item.description: If you thought the Los Angeles Dodgers would slow down on player signings because they already assembled a world champion roster, then you were wrong. According to the team on Friday, the Dodgers have added one of this offseason's most sought-after prizes: Korean star second baseman Hyeseong Kim.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Verizon to bring Super Bowl FanFest to life in 30 cities, offering NFL championship hype nationwide
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Caesars Superdome may be the epicenter for the Super Bowl in New Orleans next month, but Verizon has a game plan to ensure the championship excitement stretches beyond the Big Easy.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Postponed Sugar Bowl played with enhanced security about 36 hours after terror attack in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Georgia and Notre Dame fans packed a plaza adjacent to the Superdome and enjoyed music under clear skies — under the watch of snipers on rooftops — before filtering into the stadium for Thursday's College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Sugar Bowl. “It was a lot of fun.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: With chance for 3rd straight win, Bulls fall on road to woeful Wizards, 125-107
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After winning two games in a row to close out 2024, the Bulls seemingly had a great opportunity to kick off 2025 with a third straight victory when they visited the nation's capital on Wednesday night to face the woeful Wizards. Things certainly didn't go as planned for Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Pistons guard Jaden Ivey sustains leg injury in victory over the Magic
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Detroit guard Jaden Ivey sustained what appeared to be a severe injury to his lower left leg in the fourth quarter of Detroit's victory over the Orlando Magic on Wednesday night. Orlando's Cole Anthony slipped and fell as he and Ivey went for the ball.
item.dc:creator: DAVE HOGG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Camarillo High School to honor NBA star alumnus with jersey retirement
item.description: Camarillo High School will honor one of its most successful alumni when it retires the No. 24 jersey of Jaime Jaquez Jr. later this month.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: Gauff and Fritz put US in United Cup semifinals. Kazakhstan beats Germany to advance
item.description: Coco Gauff and Taylor Fritz powered the U.S., tournament winner two years ago, into the semifinals of the United Cup mixed teams tennis event on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sugar Bowl postponed after deadly truck attack
item.description: NEW ORLEANS — The College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Sugar Bowl between Georgia and Notre Dame has been postponed after a truck plowed into a New Year’s crowd about a mile away early Wednesday, killing at least 10 people.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Blues have all the fun in convincing victory over the Blackhawks in the Winter Classic
item.description: The Winter Classic returned to Wrigley on the final day of 2024, and the Chicago Blackhawks threw quite a party but ended up on the losing end.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Westbrook's triple-double leads Denver past Utah
item.description: DENVER (AP) - Russell Westbrook became the second player in NBA history to have a triple-double with zero turnovers and shoot 100% from the field and the free-throw line in the Denver Nuggets' 132-121 win over the Utah Jazz on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Utah's road winning streak ends in Seattle
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) - Jaden Schwartz had a goal and two assists for the Seattle Kraken, who pulled away in the third period for a 5-2 win against the Utah Hockey Club at Climate Pledge Arena on Monday. The loss ends Utah's seven-game road winning streak. “[Points] never come easy,” Schwartz said.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Darnold gives Vikings another gem with career-high 377 yards in 27-25 win over Packers
item.description: The Minnesota Vikings were ready to start their postgame ritual in honor of a ninth consecutive victory before realizing Sam Darnold was still missing from the raucous locker room because he was finishing his interview with the TV broadcasting crew.
item.dc:creator: DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Thompson-Herro fight leads to ejections of multiple players and coaches in Heat's victory in Houston
item.description: Rockets forward Amen Thompson threw Heat guard Tyler Herro to the floor to trigger an altercation that resulted in six ejections in the closing minute of Miami's 104-100 victory over Houston on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jake Evans scores for the career-high 4th consecutive game, surging Canadiens beat Lightning 5-2
item.description: Jake Evans scored for the career-high fifth consecutive game and the surging Montreal Canadiens beat the Tampa Bay Lightning 5-2 on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: ERIK ERLENDSSON Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Colorado football fans face a dramatic week across three teams
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—Colorado football fans experienced a week filled with high-stakes action, featuring the Denver Broncos chasing a playoff berth and the Colorado Buffaloes and Colorado State Rams battling in bowl games.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5
